Enum.description: The Enum module provides a comprehensive set of algorithms for enumerating over collections that implement the Enumerable protocol (lists, maps, ranges, streams). It includes functions for mapping, filtering, reducing, sorting, grouping, and chunking — forming the backbone of data transformation in Elixir.

map.description: Returns a list where each element is the result of invoking fun on the corresponding element of the enumerable.

filter.description: Returns a list of elements for which the function returns a truthy value.

reduce.description: Invokes fun for each element with the accumulator, reducing the enumerable to a single value.

each.description: Invokes the given function for each element in the enumerable for side effects only, returns :ok.

sort.description: Sorts the enumerable according to Erlang's term ordering or a custom comparison function.

find.description: Returns the first element for which fun returns a truthy value, or default if no such element exists.

group_by.signature: Enum.group_by(enumerable, key_fun, value_fun \\ fn x -> x end) :: map
group_by.description: Groups elements by the result of the key function into a map of lists.

count.description: Returns the number of elements in the enumerable.

Map.description: The Map module provides functions for creating and manipulating maps, which are key-value stores in Elixir. Maps allow any value as a key, provide O(log n) access, and are the primary data structure for representing structured data. Map literals use the %{key => value} syntax.

new.description: Creates a new map from an enumerable of key-value pairs (2-element tuples).

get.description: Returns the value associated with the key, or the default if the key is not present.

put.description: Returns a new map with the key-value pair added or updated, preserving all other entries.

delete.description: Returns a new map with the given key removed; returns the map unchanged if key is absent.

merge.signature: Map.merge(map1, map2, fun \\ fn _k, _v1, v2 -> v2 end) :: map
merge.description: Merges two maps into one, using the function to resolve conflicts when both maps contain the same key.

keys.description: Returns a list of all keys in the map.

List.description: The List module provides functions specific to linked lists, which are the fundamental ordered collection in Elixir. Lists support pattern matching with [head | tail] destructuring, are immutable, and prepending is O(1). For general collection operations, use Enum instead.

first.description: Returns the first element of the list, or default if the list is empty.

last.description: Returns the last element of the list, or default if the list is empty.

flatten.description: Recursively flattens nested lists into a single flat list.

zip.description: Zips corresponding elements from multiple lists into a list of tuples.

String.description: The String module provides functions for manipulating UTF-8 encoded binary strings. Elixir strings are binaries and fully support Unicode. The module handles grapheme clusters correctly, distinguishing between bytes, codepoints, and graphemes for accurate text processing.

split.description: Divides the string into substrings based on the given pattern (string, regex, or list of strings).

replace.description: Returns a new string with occurrences of pattern replaced by replacement.

trim.description: Returns a string with leading and trailing Unicode whitespace removed.

contains?.description: Returns true if the string contains any of the given contents (string or list of strings).

length.description: Returns the number of Unicode graphemes in the string (the perceived character count).

IO.description: The IO module provides functions for handling input and output operations in Elixir. It supports reading from and writing to standard I/O, files, and other I/O devices. IO functions accept iolists (nested lists of integers and binaries) for efficient output without prior concatenation.

puts.description: Writes the item to the device followed by a newline character.

inspect.description: Inspects and prints the item, then returns it unchanged — perfect for debugging in pipelines.

write.description: Writes the chardata to the device without appending a newline.

GenServer.description: A behaviour module for implementing client-server relationships in OTP. GenServer abstracts the common patterns of a server process that maintains state, handles synchronous (call) and asynchronous (cast) requests, and manages lifecycle callbacks (init, terminate, handle_info).

start_link.description: Starts a GenServer process linked to the current process, which will be restarted by its supervisor if it crashes.

call.description: Makes a synchronous request to the server and waits for its reply, with a configurable timeout.

cast.description: Sends an asynchronous request to the server without waiting for a reply.

Task.description: The Task module provides conveniences for spawning and awaiting concurrent work in Elixir. Tasks are used for running computations in parallel, especially for fan-out operations where multiple independent pieces of work can execute simultaneously and their results collected later.

async.description: Starts a task that can be awaited on, linked to the caller process for error propagation.

await.description: Awaits a task reply and returns the result, raising if the task fails or times out.

async_stream.description: Returns a stream that runs the given function concurrently on each element of the enumerable with configurable concurrency.

Stream.description: The Stream module provides lazy, composable operations on enumerables. Unlike Enum, Stream operations return lazy enumerables that are only computed when consumed. This enables processing large datasets or infinite sequences without loading everything into memory at once.

map.description: Creates a lazy stream that applies fun to each element when consumed.

filter.description: Creates a lazy stream that only emits elements for which fun returns a truthy value.

chunk_every.signature: Stream.chunk_every(enum, count, step \\ count, leftover \\ []) :: Enumerable.t()
chunk_every.description: Creates a lazy stream that chunks elements into lists of the given count.
